world.title: Iran proposes Hormuz deal without nuclear talks in bid to break US negotiation deadlock
world.description: As posted on this site earlier, Iran has put forward a proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and agree a ceasefire before tackling nuclear negotiations, in a bid to break the deepening stalemate with the US over the terms of a deal.

world.title: Iran gives US proposal for reopening Strait of Hormuz, ending war
world.description: The report also mentioned that US President Donald Trump is expected to hold a situation room meeting on Iran with his top national security and foreign policy team.

world.title: Working from home is linked to higher fertility - New study finds
world.description: A recent study finds that working from home is linked to higher fertility. In other words, among working people, more time at home means more births.

world.title: Marilyn Monroe’s final home at centre of lawsuit against City of Los Angeles
world.description: A couple faces millions in losses after buying Marilyn Monroe’s deteriorating former home, only to have Los Angeles declare it untouchable historic property.

world.title: Indonesia calls for stronger protection for peacekeepers
world.description: Chief Pvt. Rico Pramudia, who was injured during an attack by Israeli forces in Lebanon on March 29, died from his wounds on April 24, bringing the total casualties among United Nations peacekeepers in the country to six.

business.title: Chery's new SUV can be turned into a ute
business.description: Chery’s new family SUV can convert into a ute on demand, and it could come to local showrooms as the brand’s second dual-cab.

business.title: Google’s US$40 Billion Anthropic Bet Could Be A Game Changer For Alphabet (GOOGL)
business.description:
Recently, Alphabet’s Google confirmed plans to invest up to US$40.00 billion in AI startup Anthropic, committing US$10.00 billion upfront in cash and tying a further US$30.00 billion to performance milestones, while also promising multi‑gigawatt cloud computing capacity to power Anthropic’s models.
This deepened Anthropic partnership underlines how Alphabet is using its balance sheet and custom AI infrastructure to pull more high-value AI workloads onto Google Cloud and its in-house chips...

science.title: Scientists Find Peculiar Differences in Two Uranian Rings
science.description: The planet Uranus is a weird place. Not only does it roll around the Sun on its side once every 84.3 Earth years, it also sports a spindly set of rings corralled in some places by strange little moons. Two of those rings, the μ (mu) and ν (nu) rings are incredibly faint, which makes them challenging to study.

science.title: The world’s most powerful telescope was switched on in Chile and may finally solve the mystery of the ninth planet that scientists believe has been hidden in the Solar System for billions of years.
science.description: The Vera Rubin Telescope, installed in Chile with the largest digital camera ever built, will scan the Southern Hemisphere sky from 2025 and could locate the hypothetical ninth planet of the Solar System in the next two years, a celestial body ten times the mass of Earth proposed by Caltech astronomers to explain anomalous orbits of […]

science.title: NASA confirms, two airplane-sized asteroids to make close approach to Earth on 27 April 2026
science.description: Two airplane-size asteroids, 2026 HJ3 and 2026 HR, are set to make a safe close approach to Earth on 27 April 2026, passing millions of miles away with no risk of impact.

science.title: Scientists Need Your Help to Find Galaxies Bending Light in Space
science.description: Imagine holding a wine glass up to a candle (of course I had to pour a glass to try this.) The curved glass bends and distorts the flame, stretching it into arcs and rings of light.

science.title: Fairer disaster aid arrives just as fast with a new routing algorithm
science.description: Researchers from Koç University and international collaborators have developed a new algorithm that enables faster and more equitable distribution of disaster relief supplies. By integrating fairness directly into logistics planning, the model reduces inequality in unmet demand by up to 34% without compromising delivery speed. The approach offers a practical tool for improving decision-making in real-world emergency response operations.

science.title: Analysing microscopic life in glacial lake muds points to the changing ecosystem of Ladakh's Tsoltak Glacial Lake
science.description: Microscopic aquatic life and chemical signatures trapped in the sediments of a remote Himalayan lake are revealing how prolonged summers are subtly transforming some of Earth's harshest and most fragile environments. | Uttarakhand Open University,Birbal Sahni Institute of Palaeosciences,Agharkar Research Institute,Academy of Scientific and Innovative Research ,Tsoltak Lake | Research News by "Research Matters"

ai.title: ET Awards: Sitharaman flags AI threat and global risks, says reforms on the way to support India Inc growth
ai.description: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman is preparing new reforms to ease business compliance. She believes strong domestic consumption will help India overcome global challenges. The government is also addressing cybersecurity threats from advanced AI models. Sitharaman assures that reforms will continue to support industry growth and manufacturing.

ai.title: Apple, Google Caught 'Helping Users' Find Apps That Can Deepfake Nude Pictures of Real People, and Worse Kids Are Vulnerable Too
ai.description: Apple and Google were found to be promoting the use of apps that sexualize imagery through artificial intelligence.

ai.title: India tech giants struggle to shake off $115 billion rout
ai.description: India's top software exporters are facing investor concerns over growth, with Infosys and HCL Technologies reporting results below expectations. This downturn, exacerbated by a weak global economy and AI disruption, has led to a significant drop in the IT sector's valuation, wiping billions off its market cap.

ai.title: Costly, but AI is not yet a bubble
ai.description: Explore why the current AI investment cycle differs from past bubbles, potentially lasting longer due to cash-rich corporate backing.

ai.title: ET Awards: India Inc discusses strategy as FM Sitharaman signals readiness for reforms amid rising global uncertainty
ai.description: India's finance minister has urged industry leaders to engage in open dialogue. Discussions at the Economic Times Awards focused on navigating global uncertainties. Experts proposed measures from wooing the diaspora to harnessing artificial intelligence. The nation is seen as well-positioned to manage disruptions, with a focus on domestic demand and future preparedness.

ai.title: AI smart glasses will help visually impaired runners take on the London Marathon
ai.description: Dowler, who has Stargardt disease and says she has about 10% useful vision, only began running last year, starting with a couch to 5K program before building up to marathon distance. She is now preparing to run the London Marathon with her boyfriend as her guide, using AI powered Oakley Meta Vanguard smart glasses to help her navigate and track her progress.
